paper.paper_title: 110 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text: In the picture, the boy is       the old man.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   In the picture, the boy is       the old man.
     (A) smiling at
     (B) dancing with
     (C) cheering for
     (D) bowing to

questions.explanation: 根據圖片內容，男孩的姿態是向老人鞠躬，表示尊敬或問候，故(D) bowing to正確。(A) smiling at只是微笑，未能表現出圖中明顯的鞠躬動作；(B) dancing with表示跳舞，與圖片內容不符；(C) cheering for是歡呼加油，也不符合圖中的恭敬姿態。

questions.question_text: Listen! The baby          in the bedroom. Why don’t you go in and take a look?

source_meta.raw_text:
2.   Listen! The baby          in the bedroom. Why don’t you go in and take a look?
     (A) cried             (B) cries           (C) is crying      (D) will cry

questions.explanation: 題幹「Listen! The baby ___ in the bedroom.」中，Listen 是現在式祈使句，表示現在正在發生的動作，後句「Why don't you go in and take a look?」也暗示嬰兒此刻正在哭。根據英文時態規則，表示「現在正在進行中」的動作應使用現在進行式 be + V-ing，故 (C) is crying 正確。(A) cried 為過去式，不符合現在情境；(B) cries 為現在簡單式，無法表達「正在進行」的動作；(D) will cry 為未來式，與題意不符。

questions.question_text:
Jill is     that the city park is closed for the music festival because now she can’t
jog there.

source_meta.raw_text:
3.   Jill is     that the city park is closed for the music festival because now she can’t
     jog there.
     (A) excited          (B) proud             (C) scared            (D) unhappy

questions.explanation: 題幹說Jill因為城市公園因音樂節關閉而「不能在那裡慢跑」，這是一個負面的情況，所以Jill應該感到不滿意。(D) unhappy正確，因為公園關閉阻礙了她的日常活動。(A) excited和(B) proud都是正面情緒，不符合失去慢跑地點的失望語境；(C) scared恐懼與題意無關。

questions.question_text: Steven wants to be a      , because he loves to watch people enjoy the food he prepares.

source_meta.raw_text:
4.   Steven wants to be a      , because he loves to watch people enjoy the food he prepares.
     (A) cook             (B) doctor           (C) driver            (D) farmer

questions.explanation: 題幹說Steven喜歡看人們享受他準備的食物，因此他想成為「cook（廚師）」，故(A)正確。(B)doctor醫生與準備食物無關；(C)driver司機不涉及烹飪；(D)farmer農民雖與食物相關但重點在種植而非烹飪與享受，均不符合「watches people enjoy the food he prepares」的描述。

questions.question_text:
Paul misses his parents a lot. He        them since he came to work in Taiwan a
year ago.

source_meta.raw_text:
5.   Paul misses his parents a lot. He        them since he came to work in Taiwan a
     year ago.
     (A) didn’t see      (B) doesn’t see         (C) hasn’t seen        (D) won’t see

questions.explanation: 題目關鍵在於時間狀語「since he came to work in Taiwan a year ago」，表示從一年前開始至今的持續時間。此時應使用現在完成式，強調過去發生的動作對現在的影響。Paul 從一年前來台灣工作後就沒見過父母，這個狀態一直延續到現在，故 (C) hasn't seen 正確。(A) didn't see 為簡單過去式，無法表達持續性；(B) doesn't see 為簡單現在式，不符合「since + 過去時間點」的文法搭配；(D) won't see 為未來式，與題意不符。

questions.question_text:
Our teacher Ms. Wu seldom laughs, but when she                , everyone in the same
building can hear her.

source_meta.raw_text:
6.   Our teacher Ms. Wu seldom laughs, but when she                , everyone in the same
     building can hear her.
     (A) can              (B) does          (C) has                     (D) will

questions.explanation: 本題考查英文強調句型。題幹使用「seldom laughs」與後半句對比，需要用助動詞 does 來強調「當她確實笑的時候」。根據英文強調文法，當主句用現在簡單式且含有否定或限定詞（如 seldom），後半句應用「do/does + 原動詞」來表示強調對比，意為「當她真的笑時」。故 (B) 正確。(A) can 改變語意為能力；(C) has 時態不符；(D) will 為未來式，與 seldom laughs 的現在式不搭配。

questions.question_text: My sister is coming to my home today. She        with me for a week.

source_meta.raw_text:
7.   My sister is coming to my home today. She        with me for a week.
     (A) stays            (B) stayed        (C) has stayed      (D) will stay

questions.explanation: 句子表達「我妹妹今天要來我家，她將和我待一週」，時間副詞「today」和「for a week」表示未來事件，需用未來式。「will stay」正確表達妹妹尚未到達、將在未來發生的動作，故(D)正確。(A)stays為現在簡單式，無法表達未來；(B)stayed為過去式，與「today」時態矛盾；(C)has stayed為現在完成式，表示已完成的過去動作，不符合「將待一週」的未來計畫。

questions.question_text:
Edward had worked as a computer engineer for ten years. This       helped him a
lot when he started his own computer shop.

source_meta.raw_text:
8.   Edward had worked as a computer engineer for ten years. This       helped him a
     lot when he started his own computer shop.
     (A) chance           (B) experience     (C) hobby          (D) knowledge

questions.explanation: 題幹說Edward做了十年的電腦工程師，這個背景「幫助他很多」當他開始自己的電腦店時，空格應填入能說明十年工作如何幫助他的詞。「This experience helped him a lot」意為「這些經驗幫助他很多」，符合邏輯，故(B)正確。(A)chance(機會)與文意無關；(C)hobby(嗜好)不符合「十年工作」的描述；(D)knowledge(知識)雖相關但experience更能涵蓋十年工作累積的實務經驗與技能。

questions.question_text:
If you’re interested in our business plan,       this number and ask for Ms. Lee.
She’ll answer your questions.

source_meta.raw_text:
9.   If you’re interested in our business plan,       this number and ask for Ms. Lee.
     She’ll answer your questions.
     (A) calling           (B) call             (C) and call        (D) to call

questions.explanation: 本題考查祈使句的語法結構。句子開頭「If you're interested in our business plan」為條件子句，主句應為祈使句，祈使句的動詞需用原形動詞。空格處應填入「call」與前面的「this number」搭配，形成「call this number」的祈使結構，故(B)正確。(A)項calling為動名詞形式，無法作為祈使句的動詞；(C)項and call多餘，破壞句子結構；(D)項to call為不定詞，不符祈使句用法。

questions.question_text:
Jimmy would not get up for breakfast,             his dad had already tried to pull him
from his bed several times.

source_meta.raw_text:
10. Jimmy would not get up for breakfast,             his dad had already tried to pull him
    from his bed several times.
    (A) although          (B) because             (C) if               (D) until

questions.explanation: 本題考查連接詞的邏輯關係。句意為「儘管爸爸已經試著把吉米從床上拉起來好幾次，他仍然不肯起床吃早餐」，前後句形成轉折對比關係。(A) although「儘管、雖然」表讓步，符合邏輯，故(A)正確。(B) because「因為」表因果，若選之則變成「因為爸爸試著拉他，所以他不起床」，因果關係不合理；(C) if「如果」為條件句，不符文意；(D) until「直到」表時間，無法解釋為何不起床。

questions.question_text:
Duncan spent all his money trying to          the bookstore his mom left him. Sadly,
the business never got better, and he had to close it in the end.

source_meta.raw_text:
11. Duncan spent all his money trying to          the bookstore his mom left him. Sadly,
    the business never got better, and he had to close it in the end.
    (A) build            (B) buy               (C) save               (D) start

questions.explanation: 題目關鍵在於理解 Duncan 與書店的關係。題幹提到「the bookstore his mom left him」，表示書店是媽媽留給他的，他已經擁有它，因此不需要 build（建造）或 buy（購買）。Duncan 花光所有錢試圖「save the bookstore」，意即拯救或維持這家書店的經營，符合「花錢卻最終失敗」的邏輯。故 (C) 正確。(A) build 與 (B) buy 都暗示他沒有書店，與題意矛盾；(D) start 雖可表開設，但通常搭配新事業，不符合「媽媽留下的書店」之既有狀況。

questions.question_text: Fiona loves listening to her children sing songs         at school.

source_meta.raw_text:
12. Fiona loves listening to her children sing songs         at school.
    (A) are learned      (B) that learned      (C) they learned      (D) that they are learned

questions.explanation: 本題考查關係代名詞與動詞時態。空格前為「songs」，需要關係子句修飾。正確答案應為「that they learned」，其中 that 引導關係子句，they 為子句主詞，learned 為過去式動詞，表示孩子們在學校學過的歌曲。故 (C) 正確。(A) 項 are learned 為被動式，但子句已有主詞 they，不需被動；(B) 項 that learned 缺少主詞，文法不完整；(D) 項 that they are learned 為被動式且時態不當，應用簡單過去式表達已學過的動作。

questions.question_text:
Beverly eats lots of snacks       meals. That’s why she is often too full to eat
anything at mealtimes.

source_meta.raw_text:
13. Beverly eats lots of snacks       meals. That’s why she is often too full to eat
    anything at mealtimes.
    (A) after             (B) between        (C) during           (D) from

questions.explanation: 句意為「Beverly在___進食大量零食，所以她在用餐時間常常太飽而吃不下東西」。空格前後文脈顯示零食進食時間與正餐時間的關係，「between meals」表示「在正餐之間」，符合邏輯——在正餐之間吃零食導致正餐時不餓。故(B)正確。(A)after meals表「正餐後」，不符合「導致正餐時太飽」的因果；(C)during meals表「用餐期間」，與後句「at mealtimes」重複；(D)from無此搭配用法。

questions.question_text:
Nora: Can I check your drawer for some tools we can use?
Matt: Sure.  Take a look.  See if you can find        in there.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. Nora: Can I check your drawer for some tools we can use?
    Matt: Sure.  Take a look.  See if you can find        in there.
    (A) any              (B) it                (C) others           (D) those

questions.explanation: Matt 說「Take a look. See if you can find ___ in there」，句子結構為「See if you can find + 受詞 + in there」，需要填入不定代詞作受詞。(A) any 表「任何工具」，符合「看看能否找到任何（有用的）工具」的語意，故 (A) 正確。(B) it 為定代詞，指特定物品，但此處未提及特定工具；(C) others 指「其他人」或「其他的（已知物品）」，不符語境；(D) those 為指示代詞，需指代前文明確提及的複數名詞，但 drawer 內容未具體列舉，故皆不適當。

media.passage_text:
We’ve prepared many wonderful surprises for you at e-BeautiMed, BeautiMed’s
online store!
Surprise 1      Join “BeautiMed Friend” online for free and get 2000
BeautiMed points!
Surprise 2      Already a “BeautiMed Friend” ? Come collect your free
gift by entering your “BeautiMed Friend” card number at
e-BeautiMed before 8/31.
Surprise 3      Get 500 BeautiMed points by shopping at e-BeautiMed and
you’ll get 500 more points if you spend more than $1,000.
Surprise 4      Shop at e-BeautiMed during 7/1-8/31 and you may win $5,000
or free movie tickets!
Surprise 5      Invite a friend to join “BeautiMed Friend” and you both can
get 1000 points each!
 enter 輸入

questions.question_text: What is this ad for?
options: Teaching people how to use their BeautiMed points.
Telling people they can shop at BeautiMed on the Internet now.
Inviting people to share their shopping experience at e-BeautiMed.
Letting people know what is on sale at e-BeautiMed from 7/1 to 8/31.

source_meta.raw_text:
15. What is this ad for?
    (A) Teaching people how to use their BeautiMed points.
    (B) Telling people they can shop at BeautiMed on the Internet now.
    (C) Inviting people to share their shopping experience at e-BeautiMed.
    (D) Letting people know what is on sale at e-BeautiMed from 7/1 to 8/31.

questions.explanation: 這則廣告開頭明確指出「We've prepared many wonderful surprises for you at e-BeautiMed, BeautiMed's online store」，重點在於介紹e-BeautiMed這個線上購物平台及其各項優惠方案，故(B)正確。(A)只是優惠內容之一，非廣告主要目的；(C)未提及分享購物經驗；(D)雖提到7/1-8/31期間的活動，但這只是五項驚喜之一，不是廣告的核心目的。

questions.question_text: What will NOT get you BeautiMed points?
options: Inviting a friend to join “BeautiMed Friend.”

source_meta.raw_text:
16. What will NOT get you BeautiMed points?
    (A) Shopping at e-BeautiMed.
    (B) Joining “BeautiMed Friend” online.
    (C) Inviting a friend to join “BeautiMed Friend.”
    (D) Entering the “BeautiMed Friend” card number.

questions.explanation: 題目問「什麼不會讓你獲得BeautiMed點數」。根據材料，Surprise 1提到「Join 'BeautiMed Friend' online for free and get 2000 BeautiMed points」，Surprise 3說「Get 500 BeautiMed points by shopping」，Surprise 5指「you both can get 1000 points each」，這三項都明確提供點數。但Surprise 2只說「Come collect your free gift by entering your 'BeautiMed Friend' card number」，重點是領取「free gift」而非點數，故(D)正確。(A)、(B)、(C)都在材料中明確標示會獲得點數，只有(D)輸入卡號是領取禮物而非點數。

media.passage_text:
Joey: Lynn, have you tried Caldron? I’ve heard that restaurant is good.
Lynn: I have. I went there once for Tom’s party.
Joey: And?
Lynn: Well, I never thought I would have to cook for myself when eating out.
Joey: But it’s fun to cook your own food.
Lynn: I don’t see any fun in that. Besides, I want nice food.
Joey: So you didn’t like the food there?
Lynn: Everything tasted the same. I mean, they were all cooked in the same boiling
pot. How could they taste different? I just kept throwing strange things into
the pot. I didn’t even know what half the things were made of.
Joey: Don’t worry. Everything should be safe to eat after a hot bath in the pot.
And you didn’t get sick after that, right? Besides, you tried something
different there. Still a good experience, I think?
Lynn: I don’t think we share the same idea of good experiences.

questions.question_text: Lynn took some photos at Tom’s party. Which is most likely one of them?

source_meta.raw_text:
17. Lynn took some photos at Tom’s party. Which is most likely one of them?
    (A)                            (B)
                                                                      likely 可能




    (C)                              (D)

questions.question_text: What can we learn about Lynn from the dialogue?
options: She likes to try new food.
She did not enjoy the food at Caldron.
She changed Joey’s idea about Caldron.

source_meta.raw_text:
18. What can we learn about Lynn from the dialogue?
    (A) She does not cook.
    (B) She likes to try new food.
    (C) She did not enjoy the food at Caldron.
    (D) She changed Joey’s idea about Caldron.

questions.explanation: Lynn在對話中明確表示「Everything tasted the same」且「I don't see any fun in that」，並強調「I just kept throwing strange things into the pot」，這些陳述都表明她對Caldron的食物體驗不滿意，故(C)正確。(A)錯誤因為Lynn在Caldron確實自己煮食，只是不喜歡；(B)錯誤因為Lynn說「I don't think we share the same idea of good experiences」，明確拒絕了嘗試新食物的想法；(D)錯誤因為對話結尾Lynn與Joey的意見仍有分歧，並未改變Joey的看法。

media.passage_text:
Below is a page from Katie’s diary.
Dear Diary,                                       June 7, 2011
I was very sad to hear what happened to Woollie yesterday.
Woollie was my favorite sheep. I have a storybook about Woollie.
It says when Woollie was four, he ran away from Mr. Armstrong’s
farm, and it took Mr. Armstrong six years to find Woollie. But
Woollie had grown so much fleece that he didn’t even look like a
sheep. Mr. Armstrong decided to cut his fleece on TV so everyone
could see. Daddy said this happened on the day I was born.
Woollie’s fleece weighed 27 kg, and could make clothes for 20 large
men. I asked Daddy if his favorite vest was made from Woollie’s
fleece. Daddy said no, because Woollie’s fleece was sold to collect
money for sick kids. In the storybook, Woollie was taken to see
Ms. Stella Clark, the leader of our city then. They even had tea
together. Daddy said Woollie did meet Ms. Clark, but he wasn’t
sure if they had tea. Daddy said Woollie was put down because he
was too sick and there was no way to help him. I cried. I hope
Woollie will be happy up there in the sky.
 fleece 羊毛 weigh 重達

questions.question_text: What do we know about Woollie from Katie’s diary?
options: Woollie was taken to visit sick kids.
Woollie was sick when he ran away.
Woollie looked strange without his fleece.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. What do we know about Woollie from Katie’s diary?
    (A) Woollie was older than Katie.
    (B) Woollie was taken to visit sick kids.
    (C) Woollie was sick when he ran away.
    (D) Woollie looked strange without his fleece.

questions.explanation: 根據日記內容，「when Woollie was four, he ran away from Mr. Armstrong's farm, and it took Mr. Armstrong six years to find Woollie」，表示Woollie四歲時逃跑，六年後被找到，即Woollie十歲時被發現；而「Daddy said this happened on the day I was born」說明這件事發生在Katie出生那天，故Woollie比Katie年長十歲，(A)正確。(B)錯誤，日記明確指出「Woollie's fleece was sold to collect money for sick kids」，是羊毛被用來幫助病童，不是Woollie本身去探訪；(C)無根據，日記未提及Woollie逃跑時生病；(D)與事實相反，日記說Woollie長了太多羊毛「didn't even look like a sheep」，剪毛後才恢復正常外觀。

questions.question_text: Which picture of Woollie and Ms. Clark did Katie talk about in her diary?

source_meta.raw_text:
20. Which picture of Woollie and Ms. Clark did Katie talk about in her diary?
    (A)                (B)                  (C)                   (D)

questions.question_text: What happened when Woollie was put down?

source_meta.raw_text:
21. What happened when Woollie was put down?
    (A) He was sold.		                   (B) He couldn’t be found.
    (C) He died.		                       (D) He became famous.

questions.explanation: 根據日記內容，「Daddy said Woollie was put down because he was too sick and there was no way to help him」，其中「put down」在此指動物因病無法救治而被安樂死，故(C)正確。(A)錯誤，羊毛被賣掉但Woollie本身未被出售；(B)錯誤，Woollie早已被找到；(D)錯誤，Woollie確實出名但題目問的是「put down時發生了什麼」，答案應指其死亡。

media.passage_text:
Here’s The Piano Lesson, one of Henri
Matisse’s most famous paintings. The boy in
the picture is the painter’s son, Pierre. In the
picture, Pierre looks quite serious. Why? Well,
look at the yellow candle on the red piano. It’s
almost burned down. So maybe Pierre has
already practiced for hours. And Matisse uses a
lot of gray, not a very fun color, right? It shows
how the poor kid feels, don’t you think?
On the top right corner, you can see a woman
sitting straight on a seat. Is she Pierre’s piano
teacher? Or his mother? Well, if you’re a
Matisse fan, you’ll know this is in fact another
painting by him, Woman on a High Stool. Interesting, right? Matisse likes to do that
a lot.  In fact, there’s another work by Matisse in the picture.  Did you find it?  Right!
The sculpture on the bottom left corner!
Matisse painted this picture in 1916. Pierre was already 16 then, but the artist
painted his son much younger than he really was. Pierre
had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn’t know
whether he would come back. Maybe this is a worried
father’s way to remember the good old days.
 sculpture 雕像

questions.question_text: What can we learn about The Piano Lesson from the reading?
options: Matisse painted it when his son was away from home.
The woman who sits on a seat behind the boy is his mother.
Matisse uses yellow and red to show that his son was happy.
The candle on the piano shows that the piano lesson has just begun.

source_meta.raw_text:
22. What can we learn about The Piano Lesson from the reading?
    (A) Matisse painted it when his son was away from home.
    (B) The woman who sits on a seat behind the boy is his mother.
    (C) Matisse uses yellow and red to show that his son was happy.
    (D) The candle on the piano shows that the piano lesson has just begun.

questions.explanation: 根據文章「Pierre had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn't know whether he would come back」，馬蒂斯在兒子離家當兵期間創作了這幅畫，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，文中明確指出那位女性「is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool」，是另一幅畫作而非他的母親。(C)錯誤，文章說「Matisse uses a lot of gray, not a very fun color」來表現孩子的感受，並非用黃色和紅色表現快樂。(D)錯誤，蠟燭「almost burned down」表示已經練習多小時，課程即將結束而非剛開始。

questions.question_text: What does do that mean in the reading?
options: Put his family in his paintings.
Paint to remember the good old days.
Use a lot of fun colors in his art works.
Put his earlier art work in a new painting.

source_meta.raw_text:
23. What does do that mean in the reading?
    (A) Put his family in his paintings.
    (B) Paint to remember the good old days.
    (C) Use a lot of fun colors in his art works.
    (D) Put his earlier art work in a new painting.

questions.explanation: 根據閱讀材料，「Matisse likes to do that a lot」中的「that」指的是前文提到的行為：「if you're a Matisse fan, you'll know this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool」，即將他之前的作品融入新的畫作中。材料隨後又說「In fact, there's another work by Matisse in the picture」，進一步確認這個模式。故(D)「Put his earlier art work in a new painting」正確。(A)過於籠統，未抓住「earlier art work」的關鍵；(B)是畫作的主題而非「do that」的行為；(C)與材料內容相反，Matisse用了灰色等沉悶色調。

questions.question_text: What is NOT used in the reading to refer to Henri Matisse?             refer to 指稱

source_meta.raw_text:
24. What is NOT used in the reading to refer to Henri Matisse?             refer to 指稱
    (A) The artist.		                        (B) The painter.
    (C) The poor kid. 		                     (D) A worried father.

questions.explanation: 題目問「閱讀中哪個詞語『不是』用來指稱亨利·馬蒂斯」。逐項檢視：(A)「The artist」出現在「this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool」前的「if you're a Matisse fan」段落中；(B)「The painter」明確出現在開頭「the painter's son, Pierre」；(D)「A worried father」出現在最後「Maybe this is a worried father's way to remember the good old days」。(C)「The poor kid」則在文中指的是「Pierre looks quite serious...It shows how the poor kid feels」，此處「the poor kid」明確指稱的是皮埃爾（兒子），而非馬蒂斯本人，故(C)正確。

media.passage_text:
On Teachers’ Day, we thank our teachers for their hard work. We also celebrate
this day to remember Confucius, the great Chinese teacher from 2,500 years ago.
Well, that’s everything we’re taught about Teachers’ Day. But, the first Teachers’
Day was not on Confucius’s birthday, and it was not about thanking our teachers.
In 1930, Mr. Tai Shuang-qiu and other teachers celebrated the first Teachers’
Day in Nanjing. But there was nothing to celebrate. Teachers were paid very little
and not respected. These teachers took this chance to shout out their problems. They
had Teachers’ Day on June 6 because the date was easy to remember and near the
end of the school year. This action by Mr. Tai and the other teachers was welcomed
and followed by teachers from other cities. In 1939, the government made Teachers’
Day a national holiday. However, it was on August 27, the day when Confucius’s
birthday was celebrated. Not everyone hailed this decision: Teachers’ Day was never
about Confucius, and Confucius couldn’t speak for all the teachers either.
One funny thing was that the government was wrong about the date of
Confucius’s birthday. In 1952, people found out he was in fact born on September
28. That was when we started to celebrate Teachers’ Day on Confucius’s real
birthday.
 respect 尊敬 government 政府

questions.question_text: What idea is talked about in the reading?
options: How Teachers’ Day is celebrated now.
Why Teachers’ Day was celebrated at first.
Why Confucius was seen as a great teacher.
How teachers today feel about Teachers’ Day.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. What idea is talked about in the reading?
    (A) How Teachers’ Day is celebrated now.
    (B) Why Teachers’ Day was celebrated at first.
    (C) Why Confucius was seen as a great teacher.
    (D) How teachers today feel about Teachers’ Day.

questions.explanation: 文章主要探討教師節的起源與初衷。材料明確指出「the first Teachers' Day was not on Confucius's birthday, and it was not about thanking our teachers」，而是1930年戴雙秋等教師因「Teachers were paid very little and not respected」而發起，藉此機會「shout out their problems」，故(B)正確。(A)只涉及現在慶祝方式的片段；(C)文中未討論孔子為何偉大；(D)未涉及當代教師的感受。

questions.question_text: Which is true about Teachers’ Day from the reading?
options: It used to be celebrated on different dates.
It was started by students for their teachers.
It has been celebrated in Nanjing for over 2,500 years.
Teachers decided to celebrate it on Confucius’s birthday.

source_meta.raw_text:
26. Which is true about Teachers’ Day from the reading?
    (A) It used to be celebrated on different dates.
    (B) It was started by students for their teachers.
    (C) It has been celebrated in Nanjing for over 2,500 years.
    (D) Teachers decided to celebrate it on Confucius’s birthday.

questions.explanation: 根據文章，教師節的慶祝日期確實改變過多次：「They had Teachers' Day on June 6」（1930年），後來「in 1939, the government made Teachers' Day a national holiday. However, it was on August 27」，最後「In 1952, people found out he was in fact born on September 28. That was when we started to celebrate Teachers' Day on Confucius's real birthday」，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，因為是教師自己發起而非學生；(C)錯誤，教師節始於1930年，不是2,500年前；(D)錯誤，文章明確指出「Teachers' Day was never about Confucius」，是政府而非教師決定改為孔子生日。

questions.question_text: What does “not everyone hailed this decision” mean in the reading?
options: Not everyone cared about the decision.
Not everyone heard about the decision.

source_meta.raw_text:
27. What does “not everyone hailed this decision” mean in the reading?
    (A) Not everyone cared about the decision. (B) Not everyone welcomed the decision.
    (C) Not everyone heard about the decision. (D) Not everyone remembered the decision.

questions.explanation: 題目問「not everyone hailed this decision」的意思。「hail」在此意為「歡迎、讚揚」，故「not everyone hailed this decision」表示「不是每個人都歡迎這個決定」，(B) 正確。(A) 項「cared about」（在乎）與「hailed」（歡迎）語意不同，前者較為淡漠；(C) 項「heard about」（聽說）與原文無關；(D) 項「remembered」（記得）也不符合「hail」的含義。

questions.question_text: What does the writer try to do with this reading?
options: To talk about a problem that no one notices.
To share a fun experience that was not common.
To tell a piece of history that few people know about.
To give an example of an idea that is hard to understand.

source_meta.raw_text:
28. What does the writer try to do with this reading?
     (A) To talk about a problem that no one notices.
     (B) To share a fun experience that was not common.
     (C) To tell a piece of history that few people know about.
     (D) To give an example of an idea that is hard to understand.

questions.explanation: 文章開頭說「Well, that's everything we're taught about Teachers' Day」，接著揭露「But, the first Teachers' Day was not on Confucius's birthday」，表示作者要講述一段鮮為人知的歷史——教師節的真實起源並非源於孔子，而是1930年由台灣教師為爭取權益而創立，後來政府才改為孔子誕辰。故(C)正確。(A)錯在作者討論的是歷史事實而非「問題」；(B)錯在文章不是分享個人經驗；(D)錯在這段歷史邏輯清晰，並非「難以理解」。

media.passage_text:
Dear Traveler,
You have bought an NFR ticket online for a train that runs between White
Water City and Cloud City.
We are sorry to tell you that, because of the typhoon last month, the Sand
Town – Spring Town line will be closed between August 14 and 28 2016. During
this time, some trains will change lines and will not stop at a few stations.
There will be free buses to take travelers to these stations. Please check below
for the changes. Visit http://www.nfr.gov/ for more information.
We wish you a nice summer.
Lena Muller
National Formosa Railway
1. Trains that run between Green City and Cloud
City will take the Smoke Town – Spring Town
line, and will stop at every station. Below are
the trains that will change lines.                               White Water City
Train   New Line (from 8/14 to 8/28)
Going RL101 White Water City  Cloud City
South RL102 White Water City  Spring Town                         Green City
RL103 Green City  Cloud City               Sand Town
Going RL201 Spring Town  Green City                Gray Village        Smoke Town
North RL202 Cloud City  Green City
Black Town          Hill Town
2. The free buses for the Sand Town – Spring
Town line will wait at Green City (going south)                        Spring Town
and Spring Town (going north) stations. No
Cloud City
stop at Gray Village.
 line 路線

questions.question_text: Why does National Formosa Railway write this letter?
options: There is a train ticket sale.
A train line will be closed in July.
It will open a new train line in August.
Some of its trains will run on different lines.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. Why does National Formosa Railway write this letter?
    (A) There is a train ticket sale.
    (B) A train line will be closed in July.
    (C) It will open a new train line in August.
    (D) Some of its trains will run on different lines.

questions.explanation: 信件開頭明確指出「because of the typhoon last month, the Sand Town – Spring Town line will be closed between August 14 and 28 2016. During this time, some trains will change lines and will not stop at a few stations」，說明鐵路公司寫信的目的是通知乘客某些列車將改道運行，故(D)正確。(A)錯誤，信件未提及售票；(B)錯誤，關閉時間是8月14至28日而非7月；(C)錯誤，是既有路線改用，非開設新路線。

questions.question_text: What is true about NFR’s trains between August 14 and 28 2016?
options: No trains will stop at Spring Town.
Trains that change lines will all stop at Cloud City.
People can take the free buses to any station on the Sand Town - Spring Town line.
People can go to Hill Town on any train that runs between Green City and Cloud City.

source_meta.raw_text:
30. What is true about NFR’s trains between August 14 and 28 2016?
    (A) No trains will stop at Spring Town.
    (B) Trains that change lines will all stop at Cloud City.
    (C) People can take the free buses to any station on the Sand Town - Spring Town line.
    (D) People can go to Hill Town on any train that runs between Green City and Cloud City.

questions.explanation: 根據材料第1部分，「Trains that run between Green City and Cloud City will take the Smoke Town – Spring Town line, and will stop at every station」，綠城至雲城的列車會改走煙鎮–春鎮線並停靠每一站，而根據路線圖，煙鎮–春鎮線經過希爾鎮，故(D)正確。(A)錯誤因為RL101、RL102等列車仍停靠春鎮；(B)錯誤因為RL201終點為綠城而非雲城；(C)錯誤因為材料明確指出「No stop at Gray Village」，免費巴士不停灰村。

media.passage_text:
SYRIA
Darayya
In Darayya, a city in Syria, there’s a library, and it has 15,000 books on almost
every subject you can think of. However, it is different from any libraries you know:
It is a secret underground library, and only people in Darayya know where it is.
Over the years, war has shaken Darayya badly. Every day, houses are bombed
and people are killed. Stores are closed one after another, and so are schools. To
help the kids in Darayya with their learning, Anas Ahmad, a 19-year-old student, and
his friends decided to build a library. They built the library under the ground to keep
it safe from bombing. But it is dangerous to collect books for the library. Often,
Ahmad and his friends look for books in houses that were bombed. They need to be
careful because they may be killed in another bombing.
You may ask, “In a place like Darayya, would people be interested in books?”
“Just like the body needs food, the mind needs books,” says one library user. In the
library, people enjoy their time of reading and forget about the terrible world above,
so their life doesn’t seem so hard. Through reading, they are able to dream of a
better life after war.
 war 戰爭 bomb 轟炸 seem 彷彿

questions.question_text:
Below are the ideas that are talked about in the reading.
a. The problems Darayya has.
b. How Darayya’s library was started.
c. What makes Darayya’s library special.
d. How Darayya’s library helps people there.
In what order does the writer put his ideas in the reading?          order 順序

source_meta.raw_text:
32. Below are the ideas that are talked about in the reading.
       a. The problems Darayya has.
       b. How Darayya’s library was started.
       c. What makes Darayya’s library special.
       d. How Darayya’s library helps people there.
    In what order does the writer put his ideas in the reading?          order 順序
    (A) c b a d.         (B) b a d c.          (C) a c b d.        (D) c a b d.

questions.explanation: 本題考查閱讀文章的邏輯組織順序。逐段分析：第一段開頭介紹「It is a secret underground library」，強調其特殊性，對應 c；第二段說明「war has shaken Darayya badly」及「houses are bombed」，介紹城市問題，對應 a；第三段敘述「Anas Ahmad...decided to build a library」和「They built the library under the ground」，說明圖書館如何建立，對應 b；最後一段引用「Just like the body needs food, the mind needs books」，闡述圖書館如何幫助人們，對應 d。故文章順序為 c a b d，(D) 正確。(A) 項順序顛倒，(B) 項將 a 置於 b 前不符文意，(C) 項將 c 置於最後與文章結構不符。

questions.question_text: Why do people in Darayya go to the library even during the war?
options: They find joy and hope in reading.
They want to learn how to win the war.
Their school teachers give lessons there.

source_meta.raw_text:
33. Why do people in Darayya go to the library even during the war?
    (A) They can get free food.
    (B) They find joy and hope in reading.
    (C) They want to learn how to win the war.
    (D) Their school teachers give lessons there.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，「Just like the body needs food, the mind needs books」，且「people enjoy their time of reading and forget about the terrible world above」、「Through reading, they are able to dream of a better life after war」，說明人們透過閱讀獲得精神寄託與希望，故(B)正確。(A)材料未提供食物；(C)未涉及戰爭知識；(D)材料明確指出「schools」已關閉，圖書館是學生Anas Ahmad為幫助孩童學習而建立的替代場所。

questions.question_text: What do we know about Darayya’s library?
options: It was built during the war.
It has been bombed many times.
It was built to remember people who died in the war.
Most of its books are collected from outside of Darayya.

source_meta.raw_text:
34. What do we know about Darayya’s library?
    (A) It was built during the war.				
    (B) It has been bombed many times.
    (C) It was built to remember people who died in the war.
    (D) Most of its books are collected from outside of Darayya.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「To help the kids in Darayya with their learning, Anas Ahmad, a 19-year-old student, and his friends decided to build a library」以及「Over the years, war has shaken Darayya badly」的時間順序，可知圖書館是在戰爭期間為了幫助孩子學習而建立的，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，材料未提及圖書館本身被轟炸；(C)錯誤，建立目的是幫助孩子學習而非紀念死者；(D)錯誤，材料說「Ahmad and his friends look for books in houses that were bombed」，表示書籍來自當地被轟炸的房屋，而非外地。

media.passage_text:
Usually I wouldn’t cross the park at this time
of night. But walking around the park would take
more time, and it was raining so hard that I couldn’t
even see clearly what was right before me. So I
entered the park. And that was the first stupid thing
I did tonight.
Soon after I walked into the park, I saw a man under a tree up
ahead. My heart fell. The stories I’d heard about the park   38   into
my head at this moment. Anyone with a clear mind would just turn back.
But me? No. I did   39   stupid thing: I decided to hurry past him.
Just when I was passing the man, he raised his head and gave me the
strangest smile ever. I got scared and started running. “Hey!” the man
shouted from behind. “Wait!” He was running after me!
I ran like crazy. I had hoped the trees would keep some rain off.
And they   40  . But they also made the park look even darker. I
couldn’t see what was ahead of me. Then, I ran into something. It was
the man!
“Don’t kill me!” I cried.
“What? I   41   to give back your bag! You dropped it,” the man
gave me my bag.
Now, I felt saying that to the man was the stupidest thing I did
tonight.
 ahead 在前面
